title: ericameria viscidiflora subsp. axillaris
intro: Ericameria viscidiflora subsp. axillaris, also known as the artemisia or bigleaf chamise, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Ericameria viscidiflora subsp. axillaris include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
